seoTitle: How to Fix a Deposited Payment in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn the two-step workaround for editing a customer payment that's already locked inside a closed bank deposit in QuickBooks Online.
intro: QuickBooks Online blocks you from directly editing a payment once it's bundled into a closed bank deposit, which can be frustrating when you catch a mistake after the fact. This guide walks through the workaround: unlinking the payment, correcting it, then re-depositing it. It's for bookkeepers who need to fix an incorrect amount or account without disrupting their reconciliation history.

steps.heading: Locate the deposit in the register
steps.body: Open Chart of Accounts, find your checking account, click View Register, and scroll to the multi-payment deposit that needs fixing.

steps.heading: Unlink the incorrect payment
steps.body: Expand the deposit, click Edit, uncheck the specific payment row you need to fix, then save to release it from the deposit.

steps.heading: Correct the payment entry
steps.body: Go to the customer's profile, find the newly unlinked payment, and fix the amount, account, or classification directly.

steps.heading: Re-deposit the corrected payment
steps.body: Open a fresh Bank Deposit form, check the corrected payment line, and save it to lock the fix back into your bank register.

steps.heading: Verify the register balance
steps.body: Refresh your account register to confirm the updated values line up and the cleared status flags reflect correctly.

faq.question: Why can't I just edit the payment directly?
faq.answer: QuickBooks locks payments once they're grouped inside a deposit, so you first have to unlink the specific payment before it becomes editable.

faq.question: Will unlinking a payment delete its transaction history?
faq.answer: No, unlinking only removes it from the deposit grouping, it doesn't erase the underlying payment record.

faq.question: What if the deposit is already reconciled?
faq.answer: Editing a reconciled deposit can throw off your bank reconciliation, so it's safer to consult your accountant before touching a locked period.

faq.question: Do I need to redo the whole deposit?
faq.answer: No, only the specific payment you unlinked needs to be re-added to a new deposit; the rest of the original deposit stays intact.
